title: Base URI & Base Path Configuration
overview: Configure global default endpoints to avoid repetition and enable environment-driven testing.
whyItMatters: Tests run against multiple environments (QA, Staging, Prod). Hardcoding URLs makes tests brittle. Configuring the Base URI globally solves this problem.
explanation: You can configure REST Assured globally using static fields: `RestAssured.baseURI` and `RestAssured.basePath`. `baseURI` sets the domain (e.g., `https://api.example.com`). `basePath` sets the common API version or route prefix (e.g., `/api/v1`). You can also configure the `port`. These defaults apply to all requests unless explicitly overridden in a specific `given()` block.
keyPoints: Set `RestAssured.basePath` for the common path prefix.
Set `RestAssured.port` if using a non-standard port (e.g., 8080).
Read these values from a properties file to support multi-environment testing.
realWorldExample: You use Maven profiles or a `config.properties` file to define `env=staging`. Your setup code reads this property, sets `RestAssured.baseURI = "https://staging.api.example.com"`, and all your tests automatically hit the staging environment without changing any test code.
commonMistakes: Hardcoding the Base URI in every test, making environment switching painful.
Forgetting to clear or reset the global configuration in teardown when running different test suites in the same JVM.
Confusing baseURI with basePath (e.g., putting `/v1/users` in the baseURI).
bestPractices: Always externalize the Base URI to a configuration file or environment variable.
Use RequestSpecifications instead of static RestAssured variables if tests run in parallel and require different base URIs.
Keep endpoint paths (like `/users`) in the test method, not in the global `basePath`.

interviewQuestions.q: How do you switch between QA and Staging environments in your API tests?
interviewQuestions.a: I read the environment URL from a properties file or system property at runtime, and assign it to `RestAssured.baseURI` in the setup method.

interviewQuestions.q: What is the difference between Base URI and Base Path?
interviewQuestions.a: Base URI is the host and protocol (e.g., `https://api.example.com`). Base Path is the trailing path prefix common to all endpoints (e.g., `/api/v1`).

revisionSummary: Use RestAssured.baseURI and basePath for global, environment-driven configuration.
